sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The green didn't move! I clicked (16, 19) which is supposed to be a target for green(16,32), but the green didn't move. The state is unchanged.
Wait, let me think about this. Earlier, when I moved green(16,14), I:
1. Clicked adjacent to the green at (16, 15) to show targets
2. Then clicked on the target at (16, 25) to move the green
But now when I try to move green(16,32), I just clicked on (16, 19) without first clicking adjacent to show the targets. Maybe I need to first click adjacent to show the targets, then click on the target.
But wait, in my previous successful move, I did click adjacent first (at 16, 15) and then clicked the target (16, 25). Let me try the same pattern for green(16,32).
Actually, looking at the code, I did click adjacent to green(16,32) at (16, 33) to show targets, and the targets were at (15, 20), (16, 19), (16, 22), (18, 20). Then I clicked (16, 19) which should be one of the target cells. But the green didn't move.
Hmm, maybe the issue is that clicking adjacent to show targets also "selects" that green, and subsequent clicks on targets move it. But when I click adjacent to a different green, it deselects the previous one.
Let me try a different approach: click adjacent to green(16,32) and immediately click on its target in the same action batch.
Actually wait, looking more carefully at my earlier successful move:
1. `action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 15}])` - clicked adjacent to green(16,14)
2. `action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 25}])` - clicked on target
This worked. But now:
1. `action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 19}])` - clicked on target for green(16,32)
This didn't work because I didn't first click adjacent to green(16,32) to select it.
But wait, in the last code block, I did click adjacent to green(16,32) at (16, 33) as part of the target-checking loop. But that was after the move attempt. Let me try again: first click adjacent to green(16,32), then click on its target.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: Need to click adjacent to green first to select it, then click target. Let me move green(16,32)→(16,20) properly.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# First click adjacent to green(16,32) to select it and show targets
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 33}])
print("Clicked adjacent to green(16,32)")

# Now click on the target (16, 19) to move it
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 19}])
print(f"After moving - completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
greens = []
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        boundary = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in boundary)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in boundary)
        max_r = max(p[0] for p in boundary)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in boundary)
        center = ((min_r+max_r)//2, (min_c+max_c)//2)
        greens.append(center)
greens.sort()
print(f"Current greens: {greens}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:

After moving - completed: False
Current greens: []
